questions.correct_definition: to free from guilt or blame, exonerate
distractors: To assign blame or responsibility for an error.
To establish the guilt of an accused person through evidence.
To reprimand or criticize someone severely for a wrongdoing.
To forcefully expel someone from an organization or position.
To create an intricate and detailed plan or design.
To intensify or exaggerate the negative aspects of a situation.
To avoid a difficult or undesirable duty or obligation.
To make a difficult situation even worse or more complicated.
To accept the consequences of one's own mistakes or faults.
To seek revenge or retribution for a past injustice.
A sharp and sudden verbal attack.
The forceful expulsion of a foreign body.
A profound state of exclusion or isolation.
An excessive or overwhelming flow of information.
A deliberate deviation from a prescribed method.
The rapid onset of a severe illness.
A thorough and critical examination of a subject.
The act of making a severe and formal denunciation.
A sudden and unexpected departure from decorum.
A forceful outward thrust or projection.
Beyond remedy, irredeemable.
Easily broken, fragile.
Not believable, incredible.
A former thing, outdated.
Causing alarm, daunting.
Requiring great effort, arduous.
Having a strong, harsh scent.
Difficult to decipher, obscure.
Incapable of being extracted.
Prone to irritability, crabby.

To assign a weighty or demanding task to another.

To initiate a process of thorough and systematic renovation.

Pertaining to a long and arduous journey; a difficult expedition.
To cast out or banish from a place; to expel with force.
Requiring great physical effort or exertion; strenuous and demanding.
To clear a path or remove obstacles; making progress unimpeded.
A formal and organized undertaking; a planned exploration or venture.
Characterized by a rapid and sudden departure; a hasty exit.
Something that is readily available or easily accessible; simple and convenient.
A foot-operated mechanism or device; a pedal-like instrument.
To release or give off a gas or odor; to emanate from within.
